titleStmt.title: SII 40.262: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 262. (A.R. No. B. 307 of 1933.) Mādala, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a pillar set up before the Sakalēśvara temple.
This is dated Śaka 1072, Uttarāyaṇa Saṅkrānti.

Records the gift of 55 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp to the god Sakalēśvaradēva by Goḍisāni(Guḍisāṇi), a sani of the temple at Māmaṁḍala, who was the daughter of Pōti-Nāyaka of Prāmbaṟu. The gift was entrusted to Māredana, son of Māḍibōya for supply of ghee to the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1072 gunēṁṭi U
ttarāyana saṁkrāṁtti nimityaṁbunaṁ brāṁba
ṟiti pōtināyaku kūṁtuṟu māmamḍala go(gu)
ḍisāni nimityamu tama tallidaṁḍrulaku dha¨rmma
mugā śrī sakalēśvaradēvarakunakhaṁḍavatti
dīpaṁbunakiccina goṟiyalu 55 vī
niṁjēkoni māḍiboyuni koḍku māreda
na putrānupauvitrakamācaṁdrārakkamugā
dēvaradīpaṁbunaku nityamānēṇḍu nēyi vō
yaṁgalavāṇḍu
Digital edition of SII 40.262 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
